# Project Guidelines

## Project Status (Read First)
This repository currently contains only a minimal [README.md](README.md) and no application code or tooling.

When asked to add features or scaffold the site, first confirm:
- Target stack (static HTML/CSS/JS vs a framework like React/Next.js)
- Deployment target (GitHub Pages, Netlify, Vercel, etc.)
- Any required design system, branding, or existing assets

If the user does not specify, default to the smallest reasonable implementation and avoid introducing large frameworks or many dependencies.

## Build and Test
- No build/test commands are configured yet.
- If you add tooling, also add clear, copy/pasteable commands to the README (install, dev, build, test, lint/format).

## Conventions
- Keep changes focused; do not add “nice to have” features unless explicitly requested.
- Prefer accessibility and semantic HTML (labels, alt text, correct heading structure).
- Don’t invent a folder structure without agreement; propose one and get confirmation before moving files.
- Minimize dependencies; when adding any, state why it’s needed and how it affects build/deploy.

## Documentation
- Update the README whenever you add setup/run steps or project structure expectations.
